text:
also known as JA, is a large bipedal robot developed by Japan Heavy Chemical Industries to replace the Evangelions in Neon Genesis Evangelion. 

Profile

While still humanoid, Jet Alone is visually distinct from the Evangelion Units it was designed to replace in terms of both design and coloring. While the Evangelion's appear to be somewhat human looking, Jet Alone stands out by having extremely elongated arms with long, pointed fingers. The unit's head is also quite distinct as it is retracted deeply into the robot's upper torso, giving it a slumped-over appearance. A series of glowing rectangles occupies the space where a face would normally be. Several large rigid spines, radiating in a fan-like pattern, protrude from its back and extend over its head, raising Jet's height significantly. Finally, the colors of Jet Alone also stand out from the Evangelions as unlike them, Jet Alone features fairly muted, neutral tones as opposed to the bright, bombastic and contrasting colors of the Evangelions.

Features

Unlike the Evangelions, which have a limited internal power supply and must rely on external power cables for extended operation, Jet Alone has an internal nuclear reactor (likely a pressurized water reactor) and can function for at least 150 consecutive days. Jet Alone requires no pilot, and is controlled by an onboard computer. Radio commands can be issued from a ground station.
Being a robot and not a living being, Jet Alone cannot generate an A.T. Field. Its developer Shiro Tokita pushes aside the issue, describing the creation of an (artificial) A.T. Field as "only a matter of time." The robot is of similar size and mass as an EVA unit. Being unarmed during its test run and only appearance, its full combat capabilities were not explored.

History

Neon Genesis Evangelion

Jet Alone is seen in Episode 07, when it goes out of control during a demonstration. It is stopped after Misato Katsuragi climbs on-board with the help of Evangelion Unit-01 and attempts to delete its programming using the password “Hope” provided by Mr. Tokita, only for it to fail. Fortunately Jet Alone is saved from a nuclear meltdown at the last moment when the computer virus affecting it runs its course and reengages the robot’s safeties. Gendo Ikari states towards the end of Episode 07 that everything went according to plan, suggesting that NERV was behind the incident.

Exactly why NERV saw Jet Alone as a threat is not made clear. Gendo needed the Angels destroyed in order to carry out his version of Third Impact. It is likely he was concerned that vital funding would be diverted from his division to the Jet Alone program if the demonstration proved a success thus delaying the production of further EVA Units. Alternatively, he may have feared that Jet Alone would, at some point, be used against NERV, or that Jet Alone would become a potential threat to replace the existing EVA series, forcing him to impose hibernation on current active EVAs. Since it is likely that the JSSDF would likely have shown an interest in obtaining such EVA-equivalent combat units for themselves, it may have simply been an effort to preserve NERV's unique capability.

Other Media

Neon Genesis Evangelions

This game features a upgraded variant of Jet Alone, named Jet Alone Kai (JA改), by mecha designer Ikuto Yamashita.

Neon Genesis Evangelion: ANIMA

In the novel, another distant successor of Jet Alone is seen in the form of the Japanese Strategic Self Defense Force's Strategic Defense Force Integrated Mechanic Soldier Type-4 "Akashima".

Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon A Time

Despite the original Jet Alone not being seen during any film of the Rebuild of Evangelion film series and despite not designing the first Jet Alone, Ikuto Yamashita redesigned it again as the JA-02 https://twitter.com/ikuto_yamashita/status/1392965578407645184 , the parts of it are used to create the Evangelion Production Model-New 02α (JA-02 Body Assembly Cannibalized).

Trivia

Jet Alone was named after , Godzilla's robot partner from ' film.
Jet Alone's name is a combination of Jet Jaguar's early name, Red Alone, and his final chosen name. 'Red Alone' was created by a Japanese child as part of a contest held by Toho in 1972 to create a new character that was initially going to star in his own film. After being chosen, Red Alone was refined into Jet Jaguar, and Godzilla and Gigan were added to the film to boost sales.
In the film, Jet Jaguar is a robot created by the human protagonist, Goro Ibuki. While built for good purposes, he becomes controlled by a misguided underground civilization of ancient humans called the Seatopians, who were enraged by their surface-dwelling counterparts damaging both the surface of the Earth and their underground cities with nuclear tests. Eventually, Jet Jaguar is freed from his control and goes to assist Godzilla in fighting Megalon and Gigan. Together, the robot and the monster repel the alien creatures and create a stalemate with the Seatopians.
He was even given a brief theme song at the end of the film, performed by Masato Shimon. A longer version of the song was featured on a record released in conjunction with the film's original release.
The final design settei was done by Hideaki Anno, and it was finished on 22 June 1995.

On 03 November 2023, a card from the trading card game ' was revealed as part of the collaboration between GODZILLA vs EVANGELION. The card PC11 , features Jet Jaguar wearing the JA02 as a suit. https://twitter.com/bs_official/status/1720380307482763532 

Jet Alone was probably inspired by the Japanese nuclear ship Mutsu. Japan's first nuclear powered ship built at a significant public cost, and plagued by design flaws and public controversy.

For Neon Genesis Evangelion: ANIMA, Ikuto Yamashita, at some in its production had the idea of a detachable stealth Jet Alone to combine into a quadruple tank similar to T•RIDEN•T controled by Japanese Strategic Self Defense Force. 戦自の機動兵器。 分離合体型のJ-A 2機のステルス機が合体して雷電風の四足歩行戦車に変形する。 - Neon Genesis Evangelion: ANIMA Visual Book p. 73
